pages.text: Transmutation Scrolls are an alchemist's central tools. They turn knowledge of essence relations into two arts: $(term)Alchemical Replication$() and $(term)Alchemical Transformation$().

pages.title: Sigil Scrolls
pages.text: A Sigil Scroll replicates an item: choose the object you want to create, then use its pattern to produce another copy in the crucible.

pages.title: Equation Scrolls
pages.text: An Equation Scroll transforms one item into another. Choose the input material, and the scroll reveals the output prescribed by its alchemical recipe.

pages.title: Five Tiers
pages.text: $(li)Transmutation: 48 durability; expires at noon.$(li)Terrestrial: 120; expires at noon.$(li)Lunar: 300; expires every eight days at new-moon midnight.$(li)Solar: 750; never expires.$(li)Void: unbreakable; never expires.

pages.title: Base Scrolls
pages.text: Both use an essence, crystal, paper, and gold. Sigils add glowstone; equations add redstone.

pages.title: Activation
pages.text: Right-click while holding a blank scroll. For a Sigil, place the item you want to replicate in the right slot. For an Equation, place the input you want to transform in the left slot. If a matching recipe exists, the item is consumed and the scroll activates.

pages.title: Read Before Sealing
pages.text: $(warn)The placed item cannot be recovered after activation.$()$(br2)The activated interface previews the other side of the recipe and generates a connected hexagonal pattern of target essence slots. Their essences begin hidden as question marks. A higher recipe level generally means more slots.
